target.name: Velaura AI
target.founder: Rajiv Khemani, Barun Kar, Saptadeep Pal, Patrick Xu

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $343M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Velaura AI is a Series C semiconductor company founded by Rajiv Khemani, Barun Kar, Saptadeep Pal, and Patrick Xu. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 11 firms spanning public equities (Cerebras, NVIDIA, AMD) and private venture-backed peers in chips and semiconductors, including d-Matrix, DEEPX, SambaNova Systems, and PsiQuantum.

Two valuation methods were triangulated. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $343M total capital raised, yielding $583M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Etched ($5.0B, Series B), Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D), d-Matrix ($2.0B, Series C), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C)—produced a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range of $529M to $5.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $496M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.3B. The substantial spread reflects heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the wide dispersion from DEEPX ($529M) to Etched ($5.0B). Key uncertainties include the timing and definition of the next financing round, technological differentiation relative to stage-matched peers, and near-term product-market traction in a sector marked by significant capital intensity and technical execution risk.
